groups.context_description: Term names directed inner volitional striving toward God and spiritual goals — the will engaged in sustained effort through all available means
Term names the sustained inner volitional effort of the faith-life — contending, running, fighting as the core experience of spiritual struggle
Term names inner volitional resistance directed against sin — the striving of the will against a named inner adversary
Term names the inner volitional contending for the faith — earnest defence of what has been entrusted to the inner person
Term names the inner perseverance that overcomes in faith — the volitional struggle that achieves its goal
Term names the inner volitional striving alongside another in prayer — shared struggle as intercession
Term names the inner adornment of honour — filigree settings as a metaphor for the precious quality with which the person of character is clothed
Term names the severing of inner bonds of unity — the cutting of fraternal ties that once held community together
Term names the shattering of the inner person through affliction — spirit broken as devastation, the inner life experienced as destroyed
Term names the destructive inner character that plans wickedness — counsel as the instrument of inner malice and harmful scheming
Term names the inner consequence of disregarding divine warning — the ruin that follows upon treating the word of God with contempt
Term names the moral-economic violation in pledge-taking — inner conduct that exploits the vulnerable through use of what is essential to life
Term names the inner orientation toward wisdom — the person who seeks and receives counsel as an expression of inner humility and aspiration
Term names counsel as the expression of inner character — the quality of a person's counsel reveals the quality of their inner life
Term names counsel as the instrument of inner planning — the deliberate use of guidance in forming decisions that achieve their purpose
Term names the inner authority that directs spiritual engagement — the commanding voice that calls the person to prayer in extremity
Term names the pledge as a diagnostic of inner moral character — pledge-handling as the outer expression of inner righteous or wicked orientation
Term names the pledge as a diagnostic of inner moral character — pledge-handling as the outer expression of inner righteous or wicked orientation
Term names the reckless person's inner dissociation from consequence — the pilot/helmsman metaphor applied to the one who lies at the heart of the sea in unguarded folly
Term names the anguish of anticipatory suffering — inner distress experienced in the face of what is coming
Term names the righteous soul's inner suffering from surrounding wickedness — torment produced by immersion in a morally corrupted environment
Term names the anticipated inner dread of divine torment — the fear-state produced by the prospect of deserved judgment
Term names unceasing inner-experiential torment — the absolute absence of rest as the defining quality of final suffering
Term names torment as the object of inner dread — suffering as counterpart to pride and as the judgement that answers self-exaltation
Term names the fully conscious inner experience of torment beyond death — suffering in which the person sees, knows, remembers, and grieves
Term names disease as a condition in which the inner person encounters divine restoration — illness as the context of forgiveness and healing of the whole person
Term names the birth-moment of inner emergence — the person breaking forth from constraint into new life or new capacity
Term names the inner birth-pangs of spiritual formation — the anguish of bringing another person to the fullness of Christ
Term names the inner participation in creation's anguish — the corporate groan of all things awaiting redemption, shared inwardly by those who hope
Term names the anguish of supreme inner crisis driving intensified prayer and somatic manifestation
Term names the overwhelming pangs of extreme distress — anguish described through the labour-birth metaphor: seized, overtaking, coming upon
Term names sorrow as a sustained inner state — grief dwelling in the heart, consuming life, carried as constant companion
Term names sorrow as something overcome and reversed by God — grief that gives way to gladness in divine restoration
Term names the constraining quality of anguish and its resolution — inner experience of being hemmed in, cramped, and the divine deliverance to spaciousness
Term names the extreme distress of covenantal curse — the crushing inner and circumstantial compression of siege and covenant abandonment
Term names personal inner distress met with delight in God's commandments — anguish that does not extinguish inner faith
Term names the narrow straits of extreme inner distress — the pressing constraint from which prayer rises and God responds with spaciousness
Term names the bitterness of soul in deep distress — the inner taste-quality of grief, anguish, and sorrow experienced inwardly
Term names the bitter intensity of anger — the fierce, biting quality of wrath in action and in character
Term names the bitterness of the human condition — the taste of life as toil, futility, and moral poison under the sun
Term names the groaning that arises from inner tumult — the body's expression of the heart's distress breaking out as sound
Term names the toil and misery of human labour as inner experience — the wearing quality of work that produces no satisfaction, the burden of striving under the sun
Term names trouble and misery as an inner condition brought about by sin, oppression, or the human condition
Term names the mischief and harm done by the wicked — the trouble they produce in others as expression of inner malice
Term names the anguish of concern for another in spiritual peril — the inner distress of love that cannot rest when those it cares for are at risk
Term names the narrow distress from which the person cries to God and receives divine response with spaciousness
Term names distress as the divinely appointed condition that produces return to God — anguish as the instrument of covenantal restoration
Term names the affliction overcome in Christ — anguish as the condition the world inflicts but Christ has conquered, giving inner peace amid tribulation
Term names affliction as the ground of inner character formation — anguish producing endurance, character, and hope through divine working
Term names the anguish of shared suffering and consolation — the inner participation in Christ's afflictions as the ground of mutual comfort
Term names the anguish of the great tribulation — the maximum suffering of the end times as the context of final purification and divine salvation
Term names the anguish of being given over to enemies — distress as the covenantal consequence of rebellion, with deliverance as its resolution
Term names anguish as a reliable divine refuge — the God who is found present precisely in times of distress
Term names the distress of the righteous as the condition from which God delivers — anguish as the ground of divine rescue
Term names the anguish of crisis as the place of prayer and divine hearing — distress driving the person to call upon God with the expectation of response
Term names sudden dread as the inner experience of divine judgment — the onset of panic and desolation when God's wrath arrives
Term names the shortness of spirit that closes the inner ear — the anguish of impossible burden that renders the person unable to receive even the word of promise
Term names evil as the circumstantial expression of anguish — the distress and calamity that befalls the person as the condition from which God hides and delivers
Term names the inner recognition of one's own evil conduct — the naming of wrongdoing by the person who has done it as a moral self-assessment
Term names anguish seizing the person in the moment of mortal extremity — the sudden inner grip of distress in the face of death
Term names the anguish of post-mortem torment — intense inner suffering in the afterlife state, in full consciousness of memory and relationship
Term names the anguish of deep relational distress — inner suffering produced by anticipated permanent separation from those deeply loved
Term names unceasing anguish of heart over the spiritual condition of others — sustained inner suffering grounded in love and grief
Term names inner pangs produced by disordered desire — the self-inflicted suffering of those who love money, piercing themselves with their own craving
Term names inner anguish under eschatological judgment — intense inner-physical suffering that drives hardening rather than repentance
Term names the eschatological abolition of inner pain — the promised end of all anguish in the renewed creation
Term names distress as the condition from which people cry to God — inner compression that produces prayer and from which God delivers
Term names the anguish of eschatological judgment — the inner terror of the day of distress
Term names the divine rousing of the human spirit to action — God stirring the inner person to a specific volitional response
Term names the restraint of inner love-arousal — the guarding of love against premature or disordered awakening
Term names the inner moral stirring of the person toward righteousness or God — self-rousing of the inner life in response to moral wrong or spiritual need
Term names the groaning of inner distress and suppressed grief — somatic expression of inner suffering breaking through in sound
Term names the anguish of the shortcut — the inner impatience that drives the spirit to abandon the divinely appointed path
Term names the divine yielding to inner distress — God himself moved by the misery of his people
Term names the short-tempered person as one lacking inner governance — the outer pattern of quick anger as the expression of inner deficiency
Term names the anguish of heart that accompanies deep pastoral care — inner compression of spirit expressed in tears and written in love
Term names the collective anguish of peoples at cosmic upheaval — inner perplexity and distress as the familiar order dissolves
Inner experience of being overwhelmed — waves of death, divine wrath, or affliction passing through the inner person, threatening to engulf completely
Inner experience of being at the threshold of birth — extreme distress and exhaustion at the crisis moment, and failure to respond when the moment demands emergence
Breaking of the heart — inner person shattered by grief, expressed in embodied anguish as a sign before others
Breaking of the spirit — inner person shattered by perversion, pride, or affliction, with the spirit explicitly named as the site of breaking
Inner wound — the deep hurt of person or people that demands honest naming and resists superficial healing
Brokenness healed and brokenness mourned — God's binding up of the broken inner person, and inner grief of those who feel the breaking of others
Existential distress and hardship as experienced inner pressure
Compulsion vs willing inner orientation — freedom of will
Outcry as expression of suffering, affliction and plea for help
Outcry as communal lamentation — grief and mourning expressed publicly
Distress and anguish as experienced inner condition — fear, torment, suffering
Pain as experienced inner suffering — unassuaged, incurable, of the heart
Anxiety of spirit as inner affective state
Crushing burden as inner-bearing experience of oppression
Overwhelming inner alarm and awe-struck response
Oppression as force bearing down on the inner person — the experience of being driven, pressed, afflicted
Grief and inner pain — being grieved, distressed, or pained at heart
Disaster as context engaging inner moral response — contempt, rejoicing at another's ruin, or desperate cry
Term names the acute inner distress of the person facing suffering and death — the troubled, anguished state of the inner being at the limit of what it can bear
Term names the inner distress arising from relational concern — the person troubled in themselves because of the impact of their condition on those they love
Affliction and being pressed as inner-bearing experience of suffering
Outcry as expression of inner pain, anguish and distress — from heart to voice
Inner constraint and compulsion — the spirit pressed from within
Oppressive pressing from without — distress experienced under siege and oppression
Distress as experienced condition of troubled time
Anguish as experienced inner condition of distress and darkness
Being hemmed in, distressed and confined — the experience of inner constraint before God or suffering
Provocation and harassment as hostile inner-driven action — stirring up enmity and opposition
Narrowness and constraint as inner-bearing experience of distress and limitation
Hardness of constraint as inner-bearing experience — adversity and envious limitation
Times of distress as context of inner appeal and trust in God
The vexer as source of inner distress — provocation grieving the spirit
Being in distress, constrained and afflicted — inner experience of pressure and suffering
Iniquity bound up — stored guilt as inner moral condition
Distress as acute inner condition — the moment of extremity, terror, and desperate appeal to God
The adversary/foe as source of inner distress — enmity, reproach, and the experience of being vexed
Rivalry as relational dynamic producing inner jealousy and distress
Hardening of heart and stubborn will — inner moral resistance to God
Inner character of harshness and severity — expressed in speech and conduct
Strife and contention as inner conflict — quarrelling, dispute, and the experience of ongoing enmity
Pleading one's cause — inner appeal for justice and divine advocacy
Indictment and controversy — God's case against his people as relational-moral accusation
Bad news and evil report as occasion of inner fear, grief or sadness
Bad/harmful spirit as inner torment — the harmful spirit afflicting Saul
Evil experienced as inner-bearing suffering condition — unhappy business, grievous evil
Evil as inner moral disposition — wickedness conceived in the heart, intended, and enacted
Evil as experienced suffering — distress, harm, and calamity borne by persons
Evil repaid and returned — the moral economy of wrong done and consequences borne
Being evil and doing wickedly — inner moral disposition enacted
Displeasure and anger as inner affective response — being displeased, grieved, or made sad
Harm and calamity as inner-bearing experience — being treated harshly, brought low, or suffering hurt
Meditation and inner pondering — reflective contemplation of God's works and word
Complaint and lament as inner expression of distress
Distress and oppression as inner and social suffering
Grief and sorrow as spontaneous inner affective response
Grief caused by another — interpersonal pain
Godly grief producing inner transformation and repentance
God's forming of the inner person — heart and spirit
Potter-clay metaphor as inner-being orientation — human dependence, formation, and moral shaping before God
Being troubled/harassed as inner state of distress and helplessness
Constrained/pressed in inner being — restriction of soul and affections
Distress as existential suffering condition
Amazement as inner affective response to unexpected authority or teaching
Utter astonishment as inner affective response
Digging/plotting as expression of inner malice and scheming
Opened ear as inner receptivity to God
Oppressive pressure as experienced inner-bearing condition
Exhausting toil as inner-bearing weariness

Term names compulsion on inner religious identity and practice — external pressure forcing conformity in the domain of faith, circumcision, and covenantal belonging; the inner-being orientation of the person as the object of constraint

Term names compulsion that overrides or presses on the inner will and disposition — the person constrained to act against or beyond their own inclination; inner orientation overridden by external force

Term names the physical act of embrace as the expression of an inner state of fulfilment and devotion — the arms receiving what the inner person has waited for; the body as the outward form of inward reception
Voluntary vs compelled inner orientation in service
Cry of distress and plea to God as expression of inner anguish or urgency
Cry of grief, anguish or mourning as outward expression of inner pain
Cry of outcry against injustice, reproach or moral outrage
Contending with God — inner existential struggle and appeal for divine response
Pleading one's cause — inner appeal to God or human authority for justice and vindication
Quarrelling and contention as inner conflict — grievance, frustration, and dispute between persons
The adversary relationship as context of inner appeal and conflict
Alarm, anxiety and troubled thoughts as inner affective state
Urgent haste as expression of inner alarm or urgency
Urgent haste as expression of inner urgency or determination
Stubbornness and stiff-necked resistance — inner moral obstinacy
Harshness and rough treatment as inner character quality expressed outward
Broken spirit under harsh oppression — inner bearing of suffering
Idol as object of inner attachment, devotion and spiritual bondage
Rock as divine epithet — God as the object of inner trust, refuge and security
Rock forgotten or abandoned — inner apostasy and unfaithfulness to God
Siege as context engaging inner trust and watchful orientation
Inner compulsion, constraint, and distress as motivating or anguishing force
Being seized with fear as inner affective state
Iniquity and wickedness as inner moral quality — conceived, spoken, enacted
Trouble and toil as an experienced inner condition of life
Distress and displeasure as inner affective state
Dismay and terror as overwhelming inner affective state
Rashness and hasty inner disposition
Term names the inner-being condition of grievous weakness and wound — the pain that afflicts and weakens the person or community, which God inflicts purposively and heals
Term names the absence of grief over sin as moral failure — the inner-being indifference to national and communal catastrophe that characterises those who are at ease

Term names sickness and grief as an inner-being condition — the suffering, grief, and moral sickness borne by persons, nations, and supremely by the Servant who vicariously carries what others cannot bear
Term names the inner suffering inflicted on the person — grief from oppression, exile, and divine discipline, which God does not impose carelessly
Term names the aching pain of the inner being — the heart that aches beneath laughter, the affliction brought to God, the pain God inflicts and heals
Term names the inner-being pain and sorrow of the person — the anguish that God sees and hears, that fills the days under the sun, and that the Servant of the Lord bears vicariously
Term names the bitterness of inner experience caused by another — the grief inflicted on the inner being through relational or domestic conflict
Term names the inner-being experience of bitterness — the grief that makes life bitter, provoked by loss and divine action, and that the person names and brings before God
Term names bitter fury as an inner-being state — the rage-bitterness that drives violent action, the inner temperature of a person consumed by enraged opposition
Term names the inner-being condition of the person in misery — the one whose inner life is consumed by bitter suffering and distress, for whom the gift of life itself becomes a burden

Term names the affliction of the person or people as it is seen and heard by God — the inner-being suffering that draws the divine eye and ear, and that becomes the ground of God's redemptive intervention
Term names affliction as the inner experience of the person — the furnace through which God refines and teaches, the condition remembered before God, and the ground of trust in his word
Term names the inner-being sorrow and injury that afflicts the person — sorrows that multiply for those who follow other gods, the crushed spirit, and the wounds that God heals
Term names the pangs of conscience — the inner-being moral distress that would arise from bloodguilt, which Abigail acts to spare David

Term names the inner-being darkness of mourning — the darkened inner state of the person who goes about in grief, whose inner life is shadowed by sorrow and oppression, and who is lifted by God from that darkness
Term names grief as an inner-being state that weakens and burdens the person — the sorrow that melts the soul, the grief a parent bears for a foolish child, and the hidden ache beneath laughter

Term names mourning as the inner-being state of grief over loss, sin, or moral failure — the sorrow that is blessed, that is the proper response to sin in community, and that is commanded as the authentic inward alternative to false rejoicing
Term names mourning as an inner-being state — the grief commanded in repentance, the self-glorifying refusal to mourn, and the eschatological promise of its final removal
Term names the inward groan of the person under the weight of embodied limitation, eschatological longing, and unresolved tension — the inner-being cry toward what is not yet
Term names mourning as the inner-being state of deep grief over loss, sin, exile, or judgment — the authentic inner response of persons and communities to covenantal rupture

Term names the mourning person or mourning state — the inner-being condition of grief that is recognised, comforted, and transformed by God, whose eschatological reversal is the gift of joy in place of ashes
Term names the groaning of the person in extremis — the soul's cry under mortal wounding, heard or unheard, expressing the ultimate inner-being limit of suffering
Term names the divine roaring that demands inner response — the lion-voice of God that produces unavoidable fear, trembling, and return in the inner being of those who hear it
Term names the inner groaning and roaring of anguish — the tumult of the heart expressed as an uncontainable inner sound under suffering and threat

Term names the inner act of meditation — deep, habitual pondering of God's word and deeds that characterises the righteous person's inner life and that the heart performs both in delight and in searching
Term names the inner sounds of mourning, groaning, and scheming — the muttering of grief, the devising of violence in the heart, and the groaning of unfulfilled hope
Term names the groaning of those in bondage and extremity — the inner-being cry that God hears, that awakens his covenantal memory, and that moves him to compassionate intervention
Term names the roaring groan as inner-being expression — the cry of dereliction, the groan of unconfessed guilt, and the wail of loss that pours out of the person at the extremity of inner suffering
Term names inner musing and groaning brought before God — the deep inner reflection that burns within the person and that is offered to God as the substance of prayer
Term names the inner groaning under oppression, desolation, and judgment — the anguished inner cry when the outer world collapses
Term names the inner groaning of the faithful over sin — the grief-posture toward corporate moral failure that marks and identifies the remnant before God

Term names groaning as inner-being expression toward God — shown to be ineffective when it accompanies covenantal unfaithfulness, revealing that the inner-being expression alone does not guarantee divine response
Term names the inarticulate groaning through which the Spirit intercedes within the inner being — the place where human prayer reaches its limit and the Spirit takes over
Term names the corporate groaning of creation — the shared travail of the whole created order as it waits for renewal, within which the inner being of the believer groans in solidarity
Term names mourning as a communal or personal inner-being expression of grief — lamentation called for, performed, or transformed
Term names the act of public wailing — the outward expression of grief at catastrophic loss or the appearing of judgment
Term names the professional act of mourning chant — the skilled expression of communal grief through formal lamentation
Term names the house of mourning — the communal space where grief is shared and lamented together
Term names intense communal mourning — lamentation heaped upon a people under divine distress or judgment
Term names the inner posture of mournful devotion — the disposition expressed in walking mournfully before God as an act of piety
Term names weeping as the outward expression of grief or empathetic solidarity — an inner state of sorrow finding its voice
Term names eschatological lamentation — weeping over the fall of Babylon as the expression of grief for lost prosperity and power
Term names deep personal mourning — the inner-being state of grief expressing itself in relationship
Term names personal or communal mourning for the dead — grief expressed through lamentation, fasting, and ritual of loss
Term names mourning as a spiritual/prophetic act — lamentation called forth by divine judgment, expressing penitential grief before God
Term names the inner condition of languishing — the gradual fading of the person under the weight of sorrow, grief, or unfulfilled longing
Term names terror or dismay — the inner condition of overwhelming dread in the face of what cannot be resisted or comprehended
Term names the languishing or pining of the soul — the inner condition of deep desolation as a consequence of covenant curse and spiritual exile
Term names anxiety as an inner condition — the weight of worry or anxious concern that burdens the person in the face of threat, uncertainty, or distress
Term names the aching of the heart — the inner condition of pining, heartache that arises from distress and covenant judgment
Term names illness or loathsome condition — the state of being sick or repulsed, and the inner condition that accompanies physical debility
Term names the pangs of anguish or death — the inner-experiential condition of being gripped, bound, or overwhelmed by pain, distress, or the approach of death
Term names the relational binding through love or sin — cords used as a metaphor for the inner bonds of love, kindness, and faithfulness, or the binding power of sin and wickedness

Term names the act of profaning what is sacred — the inner-moral act of treating holy things, God's name, or the Sabbath as common, constituting a violation of the inner-spiritual relationship with God
Term names dullness or covering of the heart — the inner condition of hardened insensitivity, imposed as a divine judgment upon the adversaries
Term names torment as the inner-experiential consequence of self-reliance — the condition of suffering that follows the refusal to trust God
Term names toil or pain as an inner condition — the experience of laborious distress, whether as a consequence of the Fall, anxious striving, or the moral-spiritual fruit of one's choices
Term names pain or grievousness — the inner condition of distress arising from suffering, moral-spiritual weight, or the consequences of life under the curse
Term names the painful toil of cursed existence — the inner-experiential condition of laboring under the Fall, marked by pain, sweat, and the longing for relief

Term names evil as an inner-moral condition — the orientation or quality of the heart toward wickedness, and the inner experience of distress or sadness arising from that condition or from life's difficulties
Term names an overwhelming inner condition of sorrow — grief that penetrates and consumes the whole person, to the point of death
Term names the experience of being grieved — an inner state of sorrow arising from the moral-spiritual failure of others, particularly their hardness of heart
Term is used as a metaphor for God as the divine protector — evoking the inner orientation of trust, security, and confidence in God as shield
Term is used as a metaphor for relational intimacy — the garden as an image of the beloved in erotic-covenantal poetry, saturated with inner-relational engagement
Term is used of God's inner anguish at handing over — disclosing the divine inner conflict between justice and compassion when faced with delivering his beloved people to judgment
Term names an inner condition of sickness or faintness — applied to the heart as the inner organ, expressing grief-induced inner exhaustion and spiritual desolation
Term names faintness or sickness of the inner being — the condition of the heart overcome by grief, distress, or moral-spiritual exhaustion
Term names the act of lamenting — the vocal expression of grief or distress originating in deep inner sorrow
Term names the act of being pierced or stricken in the inner being — used of the Servant's vicarious suffering for transgressions, and of the wounded/stricken heart
The heart stricken/pierced as inner-being affliction — the language of wounding applied to the inner state of the afflicted person
The heart stricken/pierced as inner-being affliction — the language of wounding applied to the inner state of the afflicted person
Term names an inner exclamation of moral aversion — far be it! as the verbal expression of an inner orientation of horror or disgust at a morally repugnant action
Term names the condition of the wounded — the inner experience of pain, anguish, and distress expressed through groaning, arising from physical or spiritual wounding
Term is used as an image for the inner experience of grief — the mournful sound of the flute as a figure for the moaning heart
Term names the inner-experiential dimension of intense anguish — birth pangs as a figure for overwhelming spiritual-existential suffering, whether eschatological distress or the agony of death
Term names the exclamatory expression of woe and pain — the inner experience of anguish finding voice in the cry woe!
Term names mourning as an inner-relational response to loss — the condition of grief expressed in ritual practice, personal weeping, and communal lamentation
Term names the inner-moral condition of wickedness or iniquity — the orientation toward evil that characterizes those who reject God's ways
Term names sighing as the inner-expressive act of grief — the groan that gives voice to inner distress, affliction, or longing before God
Term names lamentation as the full-voiced inner expression of grief — communal or national mourning that arises from devastation and loss
Term names weeping as a noun — the state and act of weeping — across grief, penitence, prophetic sorrow, and eschatological resolution
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names weeping as the expression of intense grief over loss — death, exile, and the suffering of the inner person in its most acute form
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names weeping before God — in penitence, prayer, and covenant distress — the inner-being act of bringing grief to God and seeking his response
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names weeping as the expression of love and compassionate identification — tears shed in solidarity with suffering others or in joy at restored relationship
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names prophetic weeping — the prophet's tears for God's people, for judgment, and for the suffering of nations — the inner grief of one who sees and feels what God feels
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names the weeping of eschatological lament — the outcry of those under divine judgment — and the divine promise that such weeping will finally cease
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names tears as the visible expression of the inner person's grief, prayer, and lament — and God's attentive response to them
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names the concluded period of mourning — the weeping that has its season and passes
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names the intensity of communal penitential weeping — bitterly, before God
Term names the prophet's secret soul-weeping — the inner grief hidden from the people but present before God
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored
